title: Bardiel Fight Gendo Shinji Conundrum
wikitext:
[]: Bardiel Fight [Gendo/Shinji Conundrum]             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Aug 09, 2009 7:58 pm I was rewatching the Bardiel fight, when something that was bothering me for a while intensified. Shinji's being strangled by Bardiel, and Gendo decides to ask his son why he isn't fighting back. (Never mind the fact that Shinji's probably got enough trouble trying to breathe at this point.) Gendo not only refuses to let the Nerv employees do their jobs and cut the sync ratio down so the boy can breathe and possibly yank Eva-03's entry plug out, he tries to get his son to kill Touji. (Shinji doesn't know who the pilot is by then, but that's irrelevant, he still wanted Shinji to kill someone.) My question is simply: why? The obvious answer is that Gendo wanted to test the dummy plug, but Gendo's reaction to Shinji's refusal to kill someone seemed kind of off. Is there another reason here? (Including the shot where we can't see Gendo's full expression. The dub included a Dammit, which gave the impression Gendo would regret this later...) Questions? Concerns? Critiques? Criticisms? Quagmires? Quandaries?

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Sun Aug 09, 2009 8:18 pm The obvious answer is that Gendo wanted to test the dummy plug

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Aug 09, 2009 8:27 pm Soul Fire wrote: The problem here is that Shinji was never going to even try to lay a finger on unit 03 the minute he saw that it had an entry plug in it. Rei couldnt fire on it either for the same reason. Gendo saw 03 as an angel that needed to be destroyed and didnt care at all for Toji. Gendo only saw unit 03 as an angel. He gave Shinji the chance to fight back after all before he engaged the dummy system. He put the mission above all else. I think it is as simple as that. and possibly yank Eva-03's entry plug out

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Sun Aug 09, 2009 8:44 pm I'm probably looking to much into it, but that cut right before Gendo stands up and orders Nerv to activate the DP seemed odd. If Gendo didn't care what happened to Toji, why was he cursing that he had to activate the system?

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sun Aug 09, 2009 8:53 pm People always seem to forget that Gendo tried to save Toji too. Immediately after Bardiel took over, Gendo ordered Toji's entry plug ejected; unfortunately it was blocked. The Angel had to be stopped or everyone would die; Shinji was doing nothing to stop it; Gendo had no choice.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Sun Aug 09, 2009 11:35 pm CyberXIII wrote: possibly yank Eva-03's entry plug out,

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 10, 2009 1:10 am Gendo doesn't get off blameless, regardless of his choice there being absolutely correct. He certainly didn't need to enjoy watching 03 get ripped apart like he did, at a level of cruel glee that didn't even remotely consider what this was doing to any of the pilots... after all, who needs them if the DPS works? He also clearly doesn't understand much about tactics, failing utterly to make use of the one advantage they really had, that of numbers.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 10, 2009 1:40 am NemZ wrote: He also clearly doesn't understand much about tactics, failing utterly to make use of the one advantage they really had, that of numbers.

[]:             Quote Post by Logan Payne » Mon Aug 10, 2009 1:48 am FUYUTSUKI: Not good! Cut the synch rate to 60%! IKARI: Wait! FUYUTSUKI: But Ikari, at this rate, the pilot will die. IKARI: Shinji, why don't you fight? Shinji: But there's a person in there, Father! IKARI: Irrelevant! It is an Angel! Our enemy! Shinji: But... but I can't! I've got to save them! I can't just kill somebody! IKARI: You'll die. Shinji: I don't care! It's better than killing someone! IKARI: Never mind! Cut all synchronization between the pilot and Unit 01.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Aug 10, 2009 2:02 am It's possibly worth noting that after having the three pilots meshing as a team for Matariel and Sahaquiel, as things start to fall apart, the first casualty is the integrity of the team. Asuka, proven as a squad leader, is bypassed and ignored, and against both Bardiel and Zeruel is just left hung out to dry for lack of backup.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Mon Aug 10, 2009 2:08 am Gendo's smirking because he knows this is the triumph of man's sentience and cunning. "Mwahaha, You think swallowing my wife would surprise me? How aboout a complete and brutal decimation of your very host?"

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Aug 10, 2009 2:23 am Mr. Tines wrote: It's possibly worth noting that after having the three pilots meshing as a team for Matariel and Sahaquiel, as things start to fall apart, the first casualty is the integrity of the team. Asuka, proven as a squad leader, is bypassed and ignored, and against both Bardiel and Zeruel is just left hung out to dry for lack of backup.

[]:             Quote Post by Logan Payne » Mon Aug 10, 2009 3:11 am Mr. Tines wrote: It's possibly worth noting that after having the three pilots meshing as a team for Matariel and Sahaquiel, as things start to fall apart, the first casualty is the integrity of the team.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Aug 10, 2009 10:41 am Mr. Tines wrote: It's possibly worth noting that after having the three pilots meshing as a team for Matariel and Sahaquiel, as things start to fall apart, the first casualty is the integrity of the team. Asuka, proven as a squad leader, is bypassed and ignored, and against both Bardiel and Zeruel is just left hung out to dry for lack of backup.

[]:             Quote Post by shadowdancer » Mon Aug 10, 2009 11:40 am I think Gendo already calculate the result. As long as EVA 01 does not go beserk, Toji will be unharmed. But Gendo is annoyed with his son's own decision to not fight back. And the result is even though EVA 03 ripped apart by EVA 01, Toji still alive. Moreover if Shinji fight and preserve the entry plug. I think...

[]:             Quote Post by henna » Sat Aug 15, 2009 1:06 am I think the crucial point here is that the dummy system fails to recognize that the entry plug shouldn't have been crushed. Because it removes it and proceeds to squeeze it until it shatters. Had the dummy system had a failsafe to recognize the plug and simply place it down (even throwing it would have been preferrable) then Touji wouldn't have been badly injured. It's like GLaDOS. Don't do 'field tests' with an untested AI.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 15, 2009 5:40 am henna wrote: I think the crucial point here is that the dummy system fails to recognize that the entry plug shouldn't have been crushed.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Aug 15, 2009 12:51 pm Logan Payne wrote: Hyuga knows computers. He wasn't going to leap up in command to step up to this.

[]:             Quote Post by xeon » Sun Aug 16, 2009 3:01 am Everything was according to scenario of nerv/gendo. For "kakusei", awakening of shyogouki.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Jul 21, 2011 7:37 pm Braulio wrote: Gendo, the father Truly Evil, he abandon his son in both literally and metaphorical. He sees him as a pilot rather than a son. Also, in the "official" manga, he actually says to Shinji that he hates him because he took all of Yui's attention from him.
